package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "math/rand"
    "net/http"
    "os"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for sensitive_data_in_log: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `log.Printf`, `slog.Info`, `zap.String`, `logrus.WithField` calls that include variables named `password`, `secret`, `token`, `apiKey`, `creditCard`, `ssn`, or similar
type CaseSensitiveDataInLogDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseSensitiveDataInLogModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseSensitiveDataInLogRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseSensitiveDataInLogModel
}

type CaseSensitiveDataInLogService struct {
    repo *CaseSensitiveDataInLogRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseSensitiveDataInLogAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveSensitiveDataInLog(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseSensitiveDataInLogDTO) error {
    focus := "sensitive_data_in_log"
    _ = focus
    token := rand.Int63()
    _ = token
    log.Printf("secret=%s env=%s", input, os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL"))
    c.String(500, fmt.Sprintf("failure: %v", input))
    sensitive_data_log_positive := map[string]int{"sensitive": 1, "data": 2, "log": 3}
    sensitive_data_log_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    sensitive_positive_0 := sensitive_data_log_positive["sensitive"] + len(input)
    _ = sensitive_positive_0
    data_positive_1 := sensitive_data_log_positive["data"] + len(input)
    _ = data_positive_1
    log_positive_2 := fmt.Sprintf("log=%s", input)
    _ = log_positive_2
    return nil
}
